title: Indefinite and Definite Integration
subject: Mathematics
content:
# Indefinite and Definite Integration

## Quick Synopsis

Integration reverses differentiation and measures accumulation. JEE integration requires pattern recognition, substitution, parts, partial fractions, and definite properties.

## Must Know

- Indefinite integral represents a family of antiderivatives.
- Substitution simplifies composite functions.
- Integration by parts handles product-like expressions.
- Partial fractions work for rational functions.
- Definite integral is signed area and has strong symmetry properties.
- Improper-looking integrals may need careful domain handling.

## Formulas / Rules

- ∫x^n dx = x^(n+1)/(n+1) + C, n ≠ -1.
- ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + C.
- ∫e^x dx = e^x + C.
- ∫sinx dx = -cosx + C; ∫cosx dx = sinx + C.
- By parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du.
- Definite property: ∫a^b f(x)dx = ∫a^b f(a+b-x)dx.

## JEE Focus

- Use ILATE priority for choosing first function in by-parts, but verify practicality.
- For symmetric limits, odd functions integrate to zero over [-a,a].
- Use t = tan(x/2) only when standard trig methods fail.
- Area questions need upper curve minus lower curve, not blind integral.

## Common Traps

- Do not forget constant C in indefinite integrals.
- ln|x|, not ln x, for ∫1/x dx over real intervals.
- Definite integral can be negative if function is below x-axis.
- Substitution in definite integrals requires changing limits or back-substitution.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you identify substitution?
- Can you integrate rational functions?
- Can you apply definite integral properties?
- Can you calculate area between curves?
